name: k8s.pod.phase
definition: Pod lifecycle phase — value is 1 for the active phase (Running/Pending/Failed/Succeeded/Unknown) per pod. Aggregating with filter on phase gives count of pods in that phase. Critical for tracking healthy vs unhealthy pod counts.
absence_means: k8scluster receiver not deployed or API server RBAC missing
cardinality_footguns: Filtering by phase attribute is required — do NOT sum all phases together as it double-counts; exact phase attribute field name must be confirmed in Stage 2

name: k8s.container.ready
definition: Number of ready containers per pod (or binary 0/1 per container depending on config). A container is ready when its readiness probe passes. Use to count ready vs total containers.
cardinality_footguns: context.k8s.pod.name has high cardinality — use in top-list only not timeseries

name: k8s.container.restarts
definition: Total number of container restarts since container start (gauge, not counter — Tsuga confirms gauge type). Rising values indicate CrashLoopBackOff or repeated failures. Use increase() over time window for restart rate detection.
cardinality_footguns: Cumulative counter — raw value always grows; use rate() for restart rate; high cardinality pod/container names

name: k8s.node.condition_ready
definition: Node Ready condition: 1 when node is Ready (kubelet healthy and all checks passing) 0 when NotReady. Summing gives count of Ready nodes. A drop is a P1 incident signal.

name: k8s.deployment.desired
definition: Total number of desired (spec.replicas) pods for all deployments. Paired with k8s.deployment.available to compute unavailable replicas.

name: k8s.deployment.available
definition: Number of available (ready and running) pods for all deployments. Available < Desired means the deployment is degraded.

name: k8s.replicaset.desired
definition: Total desired pod count across all ReplicaSets. Includes both current and old RS during rolling updates — summing across RS may temporarily show 2x expected count during rollouts.
cardinality_footguns: Old ReplicaSets from deployments may inflate counts during rollouts — filter by deployment name for accuracy

name: k8s.replicaset.available
definition: Number of available (ready and running) pods in all ReplicaSets. Drop below desired signals pods not yet ready or failing readiness probes.

name: k8s.daemonset.desired_scheduled_nodes
definition: Number of nodes where the DaemonSet should be running a pod (nodes matching node selector/tolerations). This is the expected coverage.

name: k8s.daemonset.current_scheduled_nodes
definition: Number of nodes actually running a DaemonSet pod. Should equal desired_scheduled_nodes. A gap indicates pods failed to schedule.

name: k8s.daemonset.misscheduled_nodes
definition: Number of nodes running a DaemonSet pod that should NOT be running it (taint/toleration mismatch or selector change). > 0 indicates configuration drift.

name: k8s.daemonset.ready_nodes
definition: Number of nodes where the DaemonSet pod is running and ready (readiness probe passing). < desired = pods running but not serving traffic.

name: k8s.job.failed_pods
definition: Number of pods that have failed within a Job. Retry attempts before job success are included — do not treat > 0 as a hard failure indicator without context on backoffLimit.

name: k8s.job.successful_pods
definition: Number of pods that have successfully completed within a Job. Paired with failed_pods to compute job success rate.

name: k8s.cronjob.active_jobs
definition: Number of currently active (running) Job instances spawned by a CronJob. > 1 indicates concurrent runs or a stuck previous run.

name: k8s.node.network.io
definition: Cumulative total bytes transferred (in and out) per node. Direction attribute (receive/transmit) distinguishes inbound vs outbound. Use rate() for per-second bandwidth. Aggregated across cluster gives total cluster network throughput.
cardinality_footguns: direction attribute required — filter or group-by by direction; never sum across both directions without intent

name: k8s.pod.network.io
definition: Cumulative total bytes transferred per pod (in and out). Direction attribute (receive/transmit) required. Use rate() for per-second bandwidth. Identifies chatty pods.
cardinality_footguns: context.k8s.pod.name high cardinality — use top-list not timeseries for pod-level breakdown

name: k8s.node.network.errors
definition: Cumulative count of network errors (receive and direction) per node. Direction attribute required. Use rate() for error rate. Any sustained errors are worth investigating.
cardinality_footguns: Safe — bounded by node count and direction values

name: k8s.pod.network.errors
definition: Cumulative count of network errors per pod (receive and transmit). Direction attribute required. Use rate() for error rate.
cardinality_footguns: context.k8s.pod.name high cardinality — use top-list for pod breakdown

name: k8s.node.cpu.usage
definition: Instantaneous CPU usage of the node in nanocores (1 core = 1e9 nanocores). Gauge measured at collection interval. Use max for peak detection; use average for baseline. To get CPU utilization % use system.cpu.utilization instead.
cardinality_footguns: Safe — bounded by node count; values in nanocores can be large numbers

name: k8s.node.cpu.time
definition: Cumulative CPU time consumed by the node in seconds. Use rate() to get CPU utilization in cores/second. Monotonically increasing.

name: k8s.pod.cpu.usage
definition: Instantaneous CPU usage of the pod in nanocores — sum of all containers in the pod. Use for pod-level CPU trending and top-N hottest pods.
cardinality_footguns: context.k8s.pod.name high cardinality — prefer top-list; values in nanocores

name: container.cpu.usage
definition: Instantaneous CPU usage of an individual container in nanocores. Most granular CPU metric. Compare against container CPU limit/request for saturation.
cardinality_footguns: context.k8s.pod.name and container.name are high cardinality — use namespace/deployment level group-by in overview

name: system.cpu.utilization
definition: Per-CPU-state utilization ratio (0-1). State attribute: idle/user/system/iowait/steal/interrupt/softirq/nice. Filter state:idle then compute 1 - idle for total utilization %. Most human-friendly CPU metric for nodes.
cardinality_footguns: Safe — state has bounded values (5-8 values)

name: k8s.container.cpu_limit
definition: CPU limit configured for the container in nanocores (from Pod spec). Resource config value — not consumption. Pair with container.cpu.usage to compute CPU limit utilization %.

name: k8s.container.cpu_request
definition: CPU request configured for the container in nanocores (from Pod spec). Scheduler uses this for placement. Container can consume more than its request but never more than its limit.

name: k8s.node.memory.usage
definition: Current memory usage of the node in bytes. Includes all pod memory + OS overhead. Compare against k8s.node.allocatable_memory for utilization %.

name: k8s.node.memory.rss
definition: RSS (Resident Set Size) memory of the node in bytes. Physical memory actively used. Does not include memory mapped files. Lower than total usage but more indicative of actual memory pressure.

name: k8s.node.allocatable_memory
definition: Allocatable memory on the node in bytes — total capacity minus OS and kubelet overhead. This is the denominator for node memory utilization %. Stable gauge (changes only on node resize).

name: k8s.pod.memory.usage
definition: Current memory usage of the pod in bytes — aggregate of all containers. Use max aggregation for worst-case pods; average for baseline.

name: k8s.pod.memory.rss
definition: RSS memory of the pod in bytes. Physical memory footprint of the pod. Useful for trend analysis and detecting memory leaks.

name: container.memory.usage
definition: Current memory usage of the container in bytes. Most granular memory metric. Compare against container memory limit. Kubernetes uses working_set for limit enforcement but usage is also available.
cardinality_footguns: context.k8s.pod.name and container.name high cardinality — use namespace level in overview

name: container.memory.rss
definition: RSS memory of the container in bytes. Lower than usage but reflects physical memory committed. Use for memory pressure detection.

name: k8s.container.memory_limit
definition: Memory limit configured for the container in bytes (from Pod spec). Resource config value — not consumption. Pair with container.memory.usage to compute memory limit utilization %.

name: k8s.container.memory_request
definition: Memory request configured for the container in bytes (from Pod spec). Scheduler uses this for placement. Requests << limits indicates overcommit risk.

name: system.memory.usage
definition: System memory usage in bytes by state (used/free/cached/buffered etc.). State attribute distinguishes categories. Use to understand node-level memory breakdown beyond kubelet view.

name: system.memory.utilization
definition: System memory utilization ratio (0-1) per state. Filter or group-by state. 1 - idle state ratio approximates total memory pressure. More portable than raw bytes for cross-node comparison.

name: k8s.node.filesystem.usage
definition: Current filesystem usage on the node in bytes. All mount points aggregated or by device (Stage 2 must confirm attribute key). Critical for eviction monitoring — kubelet evicts pods at configurable threshold (default ~85%).
cardinality_footguns: Safe — bounded by node count; Stage 2 must confirm if device attribute exists for per-partition view

name: k8s.node.filesystem.capacity
definition: Total filesystem capacity of the node in bytes. Paired with k8s.node.filesystem.usage to compute filesystem utilization %. Stable gauge.

name: k8s.pod.filesystem.usage
definition: Current filesystem usage by the pod in bytes. Pod-level view of disk consumption (ephemeral storage). Compare against ephemeral storage limits to detect pods at risk of eviction.

name: container.filesystem.available
definition: Available filesystem space for the container in bytes. As this approaches zero the pod risks eviction or out-of-disk-space errors.
cardinality_footguns: High cardinality at container level — use top-list or namespace group-by

name: container.filesystem.capacity
definition: Total filesystem capacity allocated to the container in bytes. Paired with container.filesystem.available to understand per-container storage state.

name: NOT_APPLICABLE
definition: Kubernetes infrastructure cost is tracked at the cloud provider level (EC2/GKE node pricing) not via OTel metrics. Proxy signals for cost optimization: CPU request utilization (over-provisioned requests waste reserved capacity) and memory request utilization (over-provisioned requests waste node headroom). Use CPU Request Utilization % and Memory Request Utilization % from the cpu-memory section as cost efficiency proxies.
